Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Let's do rollback in llog catalog code, in llog_cat_new_log() we have access to both catalog log handle and plain log handle, so we can determine that index should be roll-backed. Just bump lgh_last_idx to the 1 when it reaches  LLOG_BITMAP_SIZE(llh) - 2 value, and check for llh_cat_idx == lgh_last_idx each time to check log is full.  That can be done in log_cat_new_log() before calling llog_write().

Patch Set 1:

Mike, if I finally decided to implement like this it is because with the new logic in llog_osd_write_rec() (from your changes for LU-4528) I can no longer use the update index/record choice, and with LLOG_NEXT_IDX all records are appended to the end of file, so after catalog loop-back new records will corrupt the catalog. This was the issue encountered at sites where catalog had looped-back and which had upgraded to a version where change from LU-4528 had been integrated, that has raised the fact that catalog loop-back was no longer working.

Patch Set 4:

Humm looks like the "if ((last_idx != 0 /* the very first record case in new llog */ && last_idx == cat_idx /* so new record will overlap with first used */) || (last_idx == BITMAP_SIZE - 1 && cat_idx == 0) then return -ENOSPC" construction is causing MDT startup failures with "(llog_cat.c:88:llog_cat_new_log()) lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000: there is no more free slots in catalog" errors!!!
This is really tricky part of LLOG code ... I wonder if the simplest check should not be to simply check if ext2_test_bit(((cathandle->lgh_last_idx + 1) % LLOG_BITMAP_SIZE(cathandle->lgh_hdr)) == 0 ? 1 : (cathandle->lgh_last_idx + 1) % LLOG_BITMAP_SIZE(cathandle->lgh_hdr), cathandle->lgh_hdr->cathandle->lgh_hdr_bitmap) ???

Patch Set 4:

I think the failing test causing autotests errors is "if ((last_idx != 0 /* the very first record case in new llog */ && last_idx == cat_idx /* so new record will overlap with first used */)" which should be true when all plan LLOGs have been canceled from Catalog before and a new entry has to be added ...

Patch Set 4:

After thinking more about it, I think that this can be fixed by adding the following "if ((last_idx != 0 / the very first record case in new llog / && last_idx == cat_idx / so new record will overlap with first used / && ext2_test_bit(last_idx) / catalog is not empty /" to also check that catalog is not empty. Will give it a try!

Patch Set 17:

LASSERT or LBUG is never a valid form of error handling for errors that could legitimately happen in production due to bad data from the network, from disk, or from a user.  These should return an error to the caller. Whether we decide to make a tunable to decide whether ChangeLog errors should be considered fatal or not should be handled in a separate patch. 

LASSERT is only for cases that should NEVER happen, and indicate some kind of bug in the code, or perhaps random memory corruption.

commit.subject: LU-6556 obdclass: re-allow catalog to wrap around
commit.message:
LU-6556 obdclass: re-allow catalog to wrap around

Since patch for LU-4528 a LLOG catalog is no longer allowed to
wrap around. This is a regression and it can also cause catalog
corruption (grow behind max-size/records) upon upgrading if
catalog has already wrap around.

This patch reintroduces catalog wrap around capability, and also
introduces a new test to extensively check it.
